chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Crl. Bail Application No. 212 of 2021 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge __________________________________________________________________ For hearing of bail application: 15th March, 2021 Mr. Hashmat Khalid, Advocate for applicant. Mr. Zafar Ahmed Khan, Addl.P.G. ====================== Omar Sial, J: Ghulam Haider has sought post arrest bail in crime number 127 of 2020 registered under section 395 P.P.C. at the Bin Qasim police station. Earlier his application seeking bail was dismissed by the learned 2nd Additional Sessions Judge, Malir on 11-12-2020. 2. A background to the case is that aforementioned F.I.R. was lodged by Niaz Hussain on 28-4-2020 narrating an incident which had occurred earlier that day. He recorded that he has a cattle farm and that he alongwith his son Irfan was present there at night when 7 persons entered in cattle farm and took away 5 buffaloes on the show of weapons. 3. I have heard the learned counsel for the applicant as well as the learned Addl.P.G. None from the complainant effect an appearance despite notice. My observations are as follows. 4. At the very outset learned counsel for the applicant submitted that the 5 buffaloes ostensibly stolen by the applicant are back in possession of the complainant. Learned Addl.P.G. confirms the same. As the 5 buffaloes ostensibly stolen by the applicant from the cattle farm of the complainant are back in possession of the complainant, taking a lenient view, the applicant is admitted to bail pending trial. 5. In view of the above, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000 and a P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial court.

As the 5 buffaloes ostensibly stolen by the applicant from the cattle farm of the complainant are back in possession of the complainant, taking a lenient view, the applicant is admitted to bail pending trial. 5. In view of the above, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000 and a P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial court. JUDGE 2
